title: hemerocallis fulva var. sempervirens
intro: Hemerocallis fulva var. sempervirens, also known as the orange daylily, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its vibrant orange flowers and sword-like leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and combat infections. The health benefits of Hemerocallis fulva var. sempervirens include its ability to promote skin health, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or ointments, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
